attacks.text: Put 1 random Weedle from your deck onto your Bench.

flavorText: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

attacks.text: Put 1 random {G} Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

flavorText: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.
Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.
It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.
It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

attacks.text: Take 3 {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: When heated by fire energy, its horns can get hotter than 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. Those gored by them will suffer both wounds and burns.
Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.
When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.
It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

attacks.text: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random. Do 30 damage to it.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

attacks.text: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.
This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

abilities.text: Your Active Dondozo has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.text: Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched {L} Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.
It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.
When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.
This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.
Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any Energy attached.

flavorText: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.
It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.
It can generate and release gas within its body. That’s how it can control the altitude of its drift.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may take a {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.
Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {F} Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.
This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.
Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.
They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.
By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: After stunning its opponents with the pattern on its stomach, it quickly wraps them up in its body and waits for them to stop moving.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.
Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.
It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

attacks.text: Flip a coin for each {M} Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.
Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.
People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.
It hugely inflates its stomach and sings a mysterious melody. If you hear this melody, you’ll become sleepy right away.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.
If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.
Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.
With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.
Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overacceleration attack does +20 damage.

flavorText: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

trainer.text: Each player shuffles the cards in their hand into their deck, then draws that many cards.
Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.
During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex.
Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.

It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

trainer.text: Put 1 random Basic Pokemon from your deck into your hand.
